<!--
  Default PR template. For a component pattern use ?template=pattern.md; for a skill
  use ?template=skill.md. Use this one for everything else.

  AGENT: you opened this PR; populate the fields below from the proposal you authored.
  Merging IS the ratification — there is no separate approval step, and none of these
  fields blocks the merge. The human reviewing is the disposer; they accept by merging.
  Leave a field blank only when it does not apply, and say so.
-->

## What this PR proposes

- Skill it came from: `skills/<category>/<skill>/SKILL.md`
- Related issue / project item:
- The proposal in one line: <!-- requirements, a pattern recommendation, a finding, an estimate, a section of the design... -->

## Self-check before requesting review

> AGENT: confirm each only if it holds. An unchecked box is a note for the human, not a
> block. State next to any box you leave unchecked why it does not apply.

- [ ] **Traceability holds** — each new requirement points back to the business outcome it serves, and nothing downstream contradicts it.
- [ ] **Open questions are surfaced, not tidied away** — uncertainties, "unverified — needs a spike", and dissent are written down, not smoothed over.
- [ ] **The output carries no verdict of its own** — the proposal is a `proposal` / `question` / `menu` / `halt`, not a `status`, `score`, `ranking`, or self-ratified state. The human decides; the agent drafts.
- [ ] **Findings read as findings** — any review / red-team / panel output is framed as evidence and options, not a decision already made.
- [ ] **It stays light and advisory** — no required approval that blocks a merge, no automated disposition that overrides the human, snuck in.

> If this PR touches a component pattern or a capability, stop and use the pattern
> template (`?template=pattern.md`) instead — those carry their own evidence and
> lifecycle fields and route to the CODEOWNER architect.

## Notes for the disposing human

> What the reviewer needs to weigh, and anything you left out of scope on purpose.

<!--
  Merging accepts this proposal as drafted (or as amended in-thread). The PR thread +
  commits + linked issue ARE the record of what was decided — and what was decided NOT
  to do, and why. Anything declined belongs in the thread or a linked dissent record so
  the "why not" survives.
-->
